refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link status to teleoperation control.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and alerting system.
DP3: Integrate link status telemetry with teleoperation control system.
DP4: Employ adaptive bandwidth management to optimize link usage.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover logic
Telemetry integration required for status reporting
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FR with manageable coupling; monitoring and failover are inherently linked but can be modularized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundant network infrastructure is available and reliable.
Risk of failover delay impacting availability if not optimized.
Assumes telemetry data is accurate and timely.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit DP for telemetry integration to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical network link hardware and failover control logic embedded in vehicle systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections for teleoperation data transmission and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Link status and health data sent to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as failover hardware and control logic reside here; low diffusion risk.
Receives telemetry but does not control failover; moderate containment and diffusion.
Minimal direct involvement in link availability.
Overarching system integration but not direct failover control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Placing the failover and link hardware control within the Vehicle module maximizes containment and minimizes diffusion, ensuring clear ownership and rapid response.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in vehicle embedded systems may impact maintainability.
Coordination with Teleoperation module needed for telemetry interface consistency.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts for telemetry data to reduce integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR and supports measurable availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data sharing between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Failover logic contained within Vehicle module reduces diffusion; telemetry interface is the main cross-module interaction.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry logs and failover event records is feasible within assigned IM boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of failover control in Vehicle and telemetry reporting to Teleoperation ensures allocation clarity.

recommended_revisions: Add explicit DP and IM for telemetry integration to improve modularity.
Clarify telemetry interface specifications to reduce integration risks.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, adding explicit DP and IM for telemetry integration will improve modularity and reduce coupling risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide real-time link status to teleoperation control.

revise.why: To explicitly separate telemetry integration from failover logic, reducing coupling and improving modular clarity.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
open_risks: Telemetry interface complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation.
evidence_needed: Failover event timing and success rates.
